title: Engineering Mathematics - Discrete Mathematics - Propositional Logic

questions.question:
Let us assume that you construct ordered tree to represent the compound proposition \( (\sim (p \wedge q)) \leftrightarrow  (\sim p \vee \sim q) \).

Then, the prefix expression and post-fix expression determined using this ordered tree are given as _____ and ______ respectively.



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 2 | Question: 2

Let \( ν(x) \) mean \( x \) is a vegetarian, \( m(y) \) for \( y \) is meat, and \( e(x, y) \) for \( x \) eats \( y \). Based on these, consider the following sentences :

I. \( \forall x \vee (x)\Leftrightarrow (\forall y e(x, y) \implies \neg m(y)) \)

II.\( \forall x \vee (x)\Leftrightarrow (\neg(\exists y m(y)\wedge e(x, y))) \)

III.\( \forall x (\exists y m(y)\wedge e(x, y)) \Leftrightarrow (x)\Leftrightarrow \neg \vee (x) \)

One can determine that



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 70
options: Only \( I \) and \( II \) are equivalent sentences
Only \( II \) and \( III \) are equivalent sentences.
Only \( I \) and \( III \) are equivalent sentence .
\( I, II, \) and \( III \) are equivalent sentences.

questions.question:
Consider the following logical inferences :

\( I_{1} \) : If it is Sunday then school will not open.

      The school was open.

      Inference : It was not Sunday.

\( I_{2} \) : If it is Sunday then school will not open.

      It was not Sunday.

      Inference : The school was open.

Which of the following is correct ?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 74
options: \( I_{1} \) is correct but \( I_{2} \) is not a correct inference.
\( I_{1} \) is not correct but \( I_{2} \) is a correct inference.

questions.question:
The proposition \( \sim q \vee p \) is equivalent to



Mathematical Logic: UGC NET CSE | December 2011 | Part 2 | Question: 43

Let \( \theta(x, y, z) \) be the statement “x+y=z” and let there be two quantification given as

  1. \( \forall x \forall y \exists z \theta (x,y,z) \)
  2. \( \exists z  \forall x \forall y \theta (x,y,z) \)

where x, y, z are real numbers, then which one of the following is correct?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 75
options: I is true and II is true
I is true and II is false
I is false and II is true
I is false and II is false
questions.correct_answer: I is true and II is false

questions.question:
Match the following :

\( \begin{array}{clcl}  & \textbf{List-I} & &\textbf{List-II} \\ \text{(a)} & \text{Vacuous} & \text{(i)} & \text{A proof that the implication \)p \rightarrow q\( is true based} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{on the fact that \)p\( is false.} \\ \text{(b)} & \text{Trivial} & \text{(ii)} & \text{A proof that the implication \)p \rightarrow q\( is true based} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{on the fact that \)q\( is true.} \\ \text{(c)} & \text{Direct} & \text{(iii)} & \text{A proof that the implication \)p \rightarrow q\( is true that proceeds} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{by showing that \)q\( must be true when \)p\( is true.} \\ \text{(d)} & \text{Indirect} & \text{(iv)} & \text{A proof that the implication \)p \rightarrow q\( is true that proceeds} \\ &\text{proof}&&\text{by showing that \)p\( must be false when \)q\( is false.}  \\ \end{array} \)

\( \textbf{Codes :} \)



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | December 2015 | Part 2 | Question: 8

Consider the following statements:

  1. False \( \models \) True
  2. If \( \alpha \models (\beta \wedge \gamma \text{ then } \alpha \models \gamma \)

Which of the following is correct with respect to above statements?



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | July 2018 | Part 2 | Question: 76
options: Both statement a and statement b are false
Statement a is true and statement b is false
Statement a is false and statement b is true
Both statement a and statement b are true

In propositional language \( P \leftrightarrow Q \) is equivalent to (where \( \sim \) denotes NOT)



Discrete Mathematics: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 57
